title: Frieda Takamura - Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action Oral History Project - Oral History Transcript
creator: Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action
description: Oral History interview with Frieda Takamura of the Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action and the Washington Education Association. Completed on February 25, 2019. This interview is part of the Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action Oral History Project by the Labor Archives of Washington, University of Washington Libraries. Laborarchives.org This item contains two parts: oral history audio file and full text transcription (.pdf).
subject: Japan, Japanese-Americans, immigration, World War II, US occupation of Japan, Japanese-American internment, US Department of Defense, US military, Whitman College, Walla Walla, social justice, teachers, education, teachers’ unions, Washington Education Association, Renton, teacher strikes, racial justice, students of color, Tony Lee, Asian Americans, Asian American and Pacific Islander community, achievement gap, education policy, LGBT rights, LGBT students, Robby Stern, allyship, Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action, women activists, Puget Sound Advocates for Retirement Action, PSARA, unions, workers, labor, activism, retirement, Labor Archives of Washington, University of Washington Libraries, labor history, worker stories
